title: Pinalia maquilingensis
taxonomy: Pinalia maquilingensis is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Pinalia, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Asia. The species Pinalia maquilingensis is endemic to the Philippines and is found in the wild only in a few isolated areas. It is a terrestrial orchid that grows in shaded areas of forests and is characterized by its small, white flowers. The classification of Pinalia maquilingensis is further supported by its morphological characteristics, which distinguish it from other species in the genus.
uses: Pinalia maquilingensis is a species of orchid that has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in the Philippines where it is native. Its uses in traditional medicine include treating various ailments, such as fever, cough, and skin conditions.
